title: The Hidden Cost of AI in UX: What No One Talks About
author: Sahiil Lakra
text:
Member-only story
The Hidden Cost of AI in UX: What No One Talks About
AI is making UX faster than ever but something important is being lost along the way.
Why This Article Matters (Read This Before You Continue)
This is not an anti-AI article.
This is a UX responsibility article.
AI is becoming part of everyday design work.
But UX problems don’t always show up as bugs, broken flows, or usability issues.
Some problems appear as:
- Hesitation
- Emotional disconnect
- Lack of trust
Those are harder to measure and easier to ignore.
A Familiar Story (Maybe Yours)
Let’s say you’re redesigning an onboarding flow.
You use AI to:
- Generate screens
- Improve microcopy
- Suggest best-practice flows
Everything looks clean. Logical. Modern.
Stakeholders are happy because:
- It’s fast
- It’s efficient
- It follows “UX rules”
